channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Bob Vylan takes legal action against BBC over Glastonbury performance comments
item.description: Australian punk-rap duo Bob Vylan has announced legal action against the BBC following controversy over their Glastonbury performance last summer. Singer Bobby Vylan led chants of 'death to the IDF' d...

item.title: FCMB Reports Record Profits and Pays Dividend to Shareholders
item.description: FCMB Group Plc shareholders approved a N23.08bn dividend payout for the 2025 financial year following robust profit growth. The decision was made at the company's annual general meeting, where they al...

item.title: Canada's Historic World Cup Run Ends with 3-0 Loss to Morocco
item.description: Canada's men's soccer team concluded their historic World Cup journey with a 3-0 defeat to Morocco, marking the end of their Round of 16 campaign. Despite the loss, the team's achievements throughout ...

item.title: Springboks dominate England 45-21 in Nations Championship opener
item.description: The Springboks delivered a commanding performance in their opening Test of the Nations Championship, defeating England 45-21 at Ellis Park. The victory showcased physical dominance, attacking flair, a...

item.title: Paul Pelosi Faces Criminal Charges Over Vehicle Incident in Northern California
item.description: Paul Pelosi, the husband of Democratic Representative Nancy Pelosi, is under investigation for allegedly leaving the scene of a car accident in Northern California. According to a report by The New Yo...

item.title: The Significance of EDSA: A Historical Reflection
item.description: The article explores the historical and cultural significance of EDSA (Epifanio de los Santos Avenue), a landmark in Philippine history associated with major events like the People Power Revolution. F...

item.title: TANA: A New Boutique Development in Lyneham Balancing Space and Easy Living
item.description: TANA, a new boutique development in Lyneham, offers a unique blend of house-like proportions and apartment convenience. Comprising 11 residences, the project provides four bedrooms, three bathrooms, a...

item.title: Argentina Advances Past Cape Verde in Dramatic World Cup Match
item.description: In a thrilling World Cup 2026 clash, Argentina narrowly avoided an upset as Cape Verde, playing their first World Cup, pushed the reigning champions to extra time. Lionel Messi opened the scoring in t...

item.title: Springboks dominate England with 45-21 victory in Nations Championship
item.description: The Springboks delivered a commanding 45-21 victory over England in the Nations Championship, showcasing their resilience and tactical prowess. Despite initial concerns over injuries to Siya Kolisi an...

item.title: NHS Introduces AI Triage Tool to Streamline Patient Care
item.description: The NHS has announced the integration of artificial intelligence into its app to enhance patient care by directing individuals to the most appropriate service. The new triage tool will ask patients a ...

item.title: Former Congressman Paul Pelosi Involved in Hit-and-Run in California
item.description: Paul Pelosi, husband of former House Speaker Nancy Pelosi, was involved in a hit-and-run incident in Yountville, California, according to authorities. The incident occurred on Friday when Pelosi struc...

item.title: Marcos Expresses Concern Over Potential Shift in WPS Policy Under Successor
item.description: President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. expressed concerns about potential shifts in the West Philippine Sea (WPS) policy under his successor's administration, emphasizing the need for consistency in defending...

item.title: Canada’s World Cup squad unites nation despite heartbreaking exit
item.description: Canadians celebrated their national soccer team’s historic World Cup run, despite a 3-0 loss to Morocco in the round of 16. Fans expressed pride and gratitude for the team’s performance, which brought...

item.title: Ekiti Church Attack Victims Released After Two Months in Captivity
item.description: The victims of a church attack in Ekiti State, Nigeria, were released after being held captive for approximately two months. The abduction occurred on April 28, 2026, when gunmen attacked worshippers ...

item.title: Modi Government Pushes for Constitutional Reforms During Monsoon Parliamentary Session
item.description: The Indian government is advancing plans to introduce constitutional amendments during the upcoming monsoon session of Parliament, sparking debates over political strategy and societal implications. K...

item.title: Gurugram Traffic Police Impound Bike with 63 Pending Challans Worth ₹5.45 Lakh
item.description: The Gurugram traffic police impounded a motorcycle with 63 pending challans totaling over ₹5.45 lakh, as part of a special enforcement drive against traffic violators. During a routine check at Artemi...

item.title: Tanzania Arrests Four in Mtwara for Cybercrime
item.description: Authorities in Mtwara have arrested four individuals linked to cybercrime allegations, highlighting Tanzania's efforts to combat digital crimes. The arrests underscore the growing challenge of cyber o...

item.title: Tensions rise at Trump's Great American State Fair amid weather chaos and heated exchanges
item.description: Thousands of attendees gathered at the Great American State Fair in Washington, D.C., on July 4, 2026, as severe thunderstorm warnings prompted an evacuation order for the National Mall. Despite initi...

item.title: Canada, Mexico, and USA Advance in World Cup Tournament
item.description: The article highlights the strong performances of Canada, Mexico, and the USA in the FIFA World Cup, which is co-hosted by these three nations. Canada made history by reaching the knockout stages with...

item.title: France Advances to Quarter-Finals After Close World Cup Match Against Paraguay
item.description: France secured a hard-fought 1-0 victory over Paraguay in the World Cup 2026 last-16 clash, with Kylian Mbappe's penalty in the second half proving decisive. The match, held in Philadelphia, was marke...
